segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The mountain smelled of hot stone and a kiln that had just shut its mouth. Caelin woke to Vex's palm over his mouth, her other hand clamped to his shoulder.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "Don't make a sound,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she breathed.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "Company."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale in his right forearm tightened its pulse, not warning, not ease -- just braced. Coal-glow slid toward ember-red; the fine obsidian filaments beneath his skin crawled up toward the elbow like roots searching stone.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: From the lower passage -- the one they'd marked with chalk and left closed -- voices braided the air. Wrong, guttural, harmonized like things that had practiced speaking together inside the same throat.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "Ember-born..."

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "...the chain calls..."

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "...submit or be consumed..."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's hand left his mouth.

segments.speaker: unknown
segments.text: "Up,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she hissed, and the camp came alive in a soft clatter of straps and breath.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana rolled to her feet, sword in one hand, the other kindling silver-gold. The light stuttered gold?silver?gold -- a tremor she smoothed with breath. Thornik's pack went from hum to urgent whine as needles spun.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen's vine-marks dimmed to a determined green. Nyxara stood in a movement that wasn't one, purple aura guttering like a candle in wind. Durgan took the choke point, blades bare, shadow pooled neat at his boots -- too neat.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Signatures?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex murmured.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik checked a gauge that vibrated his teeth.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Multiple. Same resonance as Thornwick -- stronger. Focused."

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "They'll smell the Ember,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara said, eyes on Caelin's arm.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "A bonfire in a dark valley."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Can you dim it?"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana asked, already moving to shield the center.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "No."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He'd tried. White sparks punished lies, and it punished denial just as fast.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Then we work with it,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Caelin center-back. I hold the line. Thornik on my flank, steel first, toys second. Vex wide right. Elowen disrupt their approach. Nyxara -- "

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "I'll... persuade,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara murmured, voice a soft knife.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan didn't wait for orders.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "They know exactly where we are,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, jaw tight.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Kill fast, before the trap closes."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The voices fell silent. The air drew a breath.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: By first light that never reached this deep, the tunnels had learned their names.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The hum settled to a floor-note you only noticed when it stopped.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It stopped.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Dust lifted off the stone in slow spirals, then hung as if waiting to be told where to fall.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex's hand found the wall.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Metallic again,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she murmured.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Coin on the tongue."

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "The pull's stronger,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan said. Three words. Nothing more.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana's wardline thinned to chalk-pale, then steadied.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin felt the scale tighten -- warning without pain, the kind that comes just before it hurts.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The chamber ahead was the shape of a held breath.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Shadow clung to the facets and then decided to stand.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Five of them -- wrong-jointed, glass-slick, learning their limbs like newborns. They moved like puppets given too many strings. Joints bent a fraction wrong.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Skin was the color of cold parchment, stretched and shifting over bones that didn't respect anatomy. Eyes were black from lid to lid -- no iris, no white, just void that stared back.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Their shadows moved first, crawling toward Caelin's arm with hungry, fingered curiosity.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: When they spoke, three voices braided through each throat: the people they had been, something ancient and patient, and beneath both the grind of stone on stone.

segments.speaker: nyxara
segments.text: "Ember-born,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: they said together.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "The Seal remembers. The Seal hungers."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Line,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said, sword low.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Light, then edges. No heroes."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik palmed two charges.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Edges I have. Heroes, sadly, I left at home."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Left,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex snapped, already moving.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Right,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara echoed, voice silk over wire.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin took center because the path in his bones put him there.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale's edge bloomed black-violet -- ink spreading through obsidian water -- and snapped back to red-gold. It hurt different this time: not the compressive burn of claiming, not the constant ache he could now ignore.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: This was a hook, barbed and patient, tugging along the filament lattice as if it could pull the Ember out of him with steady, surgical malice.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He staggered. Elowen caught his shoulder.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I can sense it,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, voice tight.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "A severing. I can't ease this."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Then we break the hand holding the hook,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said, and ran.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He drew heat and starved it to a hard white seam across his palm, then cut the closest shadow at the ankle.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It shrieked in three registers and folded -- but did not die.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Pain lit the scale a murderous red. He kept his hand closed anyway.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana hit them like a wall of weather. Shield-first, a ward-wave plowing the nearest thrall backward so hard its shadow recoiled, hissing. Her sword came down in a clean kill-line --

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her lips moved -- automatic prayer, the kind learned in bone.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The answer struck from two mouths at once.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Protect the flame.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Break the vessel to save the flame.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her wardlight guttered, flared, guttered. Blood salted her tongue.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Choose,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she hissed -- to the voices, to herself. The blade came up anyway.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Fine. I choose now."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She stepped in and set her sword through the thing's shoulder -- and met resistance like cutting water that didn't agree to be cut. Light seared black flesh; it didn't fall. It grinned too wide -- teeth blooming like fungus where a mouth should stop.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Hard to kill!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she called.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "News flash,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said, already gone from where she'd spoken.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Duck,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said, and rolled a thimble charge.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: It coughed instead of boomed; the echo slapped the chamber flat.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The shadow's edge tore like wet paper where the sound hit it.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Acoustic shear,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he breathed.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Repeatable."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She was a blur on the fringe: two quick stabs behind a knee and a tendon, twist and away. The thrall buckled correctly -- then its shadow lashed like a whip and caught her across the ribs.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex hit stone, slid, swore without wasting air.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Shadows bite! Noted!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex went low, a fast insult of steel at what passed for a heel.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Trip the legs, quiet the mouth."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara didn't draw. She spoke a word that bent the lantern-angle; light arrived from the wrong direction.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The shadow flinched from illumination that shouldn't exist there.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Transposition,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, almost kindly.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Stand in your proper place."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik roared -- a sound that came from under mountains -- and hit the hamstrung thrall with two axes like punctuation. Steel met neck. Head rolled. Its shadow screamed like glass breaking underwater and blew away.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "One!"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he bellowed, beard alight with reflected ember.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara didn't shout. She breathed, and indecision bloomed in two corrupted minds. One raked at the wrong air where Caelin had stood; the other hesitated in a three-second loop of doubt.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Now,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, soft but absolute.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana obliged. Silver-gold through the throat; the shadow boiled like ice in summer and was gone.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The fourth rose for Vex's throat.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan was there. No shout, no flourish -- steel where it had to be. His forearm took the rake that would have opened her.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He shoved it back two steps and did not pursue.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The last turned from the fighters and stretched too many fingers toward Caelin.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Ember-born,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: three voices purred.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "The chain is mercy. Let us hold your fire. We will take the pain away."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The scale chose.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Heat flowered in his chest like a furnace door thrown wide. It wasn't temperature; it was pressure finding a path. His right arm lifted with a puppet's surety. Obsidian hex flared white. The lattice lit like molten wire from palm to collarbone.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He didn't aim. Didn't choose. The Ember chose him as an answer.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Red-gold plasma ripped the air. Granite vitrified to glossy black where it touched. The smell hit hard -- hot glass, burnt ozone, iron filings on the tongue.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The thrall didn't burn. It unmade. Flesh evaporated. Bone sifted to ash. The shadow screamed in three notes and was not.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Three seconds. Then the door slammed shut.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Pain took the room.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The remaining shadows collapsed into behaving dark, dragging cold with them that wasn't temperature so much as absence.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They said nothing more. They only learned the shape of the group and thinned into the walls.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Sound returned in layers -- first to the floor, then to the air, then, last of all, to breath.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin flexed scorched fingers. The scale tallied pain and settled to coal-glow.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Sound wavered, distant and too loud. His heart rattled a panicked rhythm. The plate in his arm dropped from white-hot to furious red, the filaments pulsing like infected seams. His lungs forgot how. He went to a knee on a floor that looked like a mirror still cooling.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen was there, palms hovering. Green light reached for him -- and guttered, the smell of scorched leaves sharp and wrong. A thin edge of one vine-mark blackened on her skin as she pulled back.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I can't,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she whispered, horrified.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Post-cast residue burns my connection. I can ease your baseline, not this."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "S'okay,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he managed through his teeth.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "You told me."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex slid in hard, fingers at his pulse, pupils, breath.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Not dying,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said briskly.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Shouldn't cast again for hours -- maybe a day -- unless we want him to."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She made a leveling motion with her hand.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "You know."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her joke was a little too thin to be a joke. He almost laughed anyway.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana kept her hand on the ward until it stopped shaking. When she finally sheathed the blade, her mouth was set against words.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex wiped her knife clean and didn't thank Durgan. She didn't not thank him, either.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara smoothed her coat as if dirt were an opinion, then watched Serana a fraction too long.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik counted fuses he hadn't used and grinned like a man who'd just learned his math could kill.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He glanced at Caelin's arm -- coal-glow again, the ember-motes drifting slow.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Back to baseline,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, as if noting a reading.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Coal means steady. Ember-red means spent. Black-violet means -- "

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He stopped himself.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Well. We know what black-violet means."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: At the mouth of the passage, Durgan hadn't moved an inch. He kept the retreat open, blades ready -- eyes trained not on the dark beyond, but on the lattice burning under Caelin's skin. His shadow flexed like a dog that had caught a scent.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He flattened it with will and left crescent bruises through his trousers where his fingers anchored him.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The hum returned, deeper, almost pleased.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Not a voice,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said, listening to the stone.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Not yet."
